item.title: Cort Rithimic 5 Jeff Berlin, Natur, 5-Saiter, Ladendemo — $1,101.30
item.description:
$1,101.30 · Mint · Musikladen

LADENDEMO MIT LEICHTEN SPIELSPUREN ---4,1 kg------------------------ Die Rithimic Serie ist von Jeff Berlin inspiriert und konzipiert. Jeff Berlin ist als einer der größten Bassisten der Welt bekannt. Cort Guitars und Jeff Berlin haben den Bass gemeinsam zum ersten Mal im Jahr 2014 veröffentlicht. Die Bässe haben einen einfachen Vintage Klang, jedoch sind sie aus sehr besonderem Holz gefertigt. Der Bass verfügt über einen Korpus aus Erle mit einer schönen exotischen Holzplatte. Features des Korpusses sind ein Paar von Bartolini Jeff Berlin benutzerdefinierte Soapbar Pickups, ein Babicz Full Contact Hardware Steg, ein Lautstärkeregler, ein Balance-Regler, ein Klangregler, passive Elektronik, und eine Gold-Hardware. Der Ahorn Hals ist mit einem Palisander-Griffbrett und vier Inline-Kopfplatten mit Hipshot Ultralight Tunern ergänzt. Erle Korpus mit Walnuss-Knoten Decke Der individuell gestaltete Korpus, mit dem schön visualisierten Ahorn und der Padauk Decke, machen ihn zu einem außergewöhnlichen Instrument. Der Korpus, aus Erle, hat seine eigene einzigartige Klarheit und Helligkeit. Bartolini Jeff Berlin Custom Soapbar Pickups Die vorhandenen benutzerdefinierten Soapbar Pickups, für den Hals und die Bridge, bieten einen warmen, tiefen Ton mit einem Klavier-wie Low-End. Die Pickups stehen für ein Splitcoil Design, mit einer gut definierten Magnetfeld Öffnung für Klarheit beim Klangbild. Babicz FCH5 Gold Bridge Die Vollkontakt -Hardware-Technologie führt zu einem erhöhten Dynamikbereich, Sustain und Auftriebs Stabilität. Die Saitenlage und die Intonations Anpassungen bleiben sicher; auch wenn Saiten entfernt werden. Hipshot Ultralite Mechaniken Die Rithimic Bässe sind mit Hipshot Ultralight Tunern (klassisches 20: 1 Tuning-Verhältnis) ausgestattet, welche aus Zinkguss mit Aluminium Saiten, Pfosten und Befestigungsmuttern hergestellt werden. 30% Gewichtsersparnis gegenüber anderen regulären Tuner führen zu einer verbesserten Balance und Tuning-Genauigkeit. KONSTRUKTION Geschraubter Hals CUTAWAY Doppeltes Cutaway KORPUS FORM Erle Korpus mit Walnuss-Knoten Decke HALS Kanadischer Ahorn Hals GRIFFBRETT Ebenholz MENSUR 34" (864mm) EINLAGEN Abalone MECHANIKEN Hipshot Ultralite Mechaniken BRÜCKE Babicz FCH5 Bridge TONABNEHMER Bartolini Jeff Berlin Custom Soapbar Pickups ELEKTRONIK 1 Volume, 1 Balance, 1 Tone passive Electronics HARDWARE Gold SAITEN DR DDT(40~120) Saiten

item.title: Ibanez SR600E Cosmic Blue Starburst Flat Bass Guitar Pre-Owned — $783.11
item.description:
$783.11 · Excellent · The Rock Inn

Pre-owned in excellent condition, this Ibanez SR600E is finished in a striking Cosmic Blue Starburst Flat finish which looks fantastic over an Ash body. This is a great playing bass and features Nordstrand pickups from factory.

There are minimal signs of wear on this bass, a great find for the new buyer looking for a bargain. The frets are also in great condition with only a small amount of wear.

A great bass guitar with plenty of tonal versatility on tap.

5pc Jatoba/Walnut neck

The 5pc Jatoba/Walnut neck provides tonal clarity and superior attack.

Rosewood fretboard

Rosewood provides a well-balanced solid tone with a focused mid range.

Nordstrand™ Big Break pickups

Nordstrand™ "Big Break" pickups cram a meaty split coil into a soapbar-style pickup, producing a full-bodied, powerful, aggressive tonal texture, while still retaining the clarity that's characteristic of single coil tone with noiseless performance.

Accu-cast B500 bridge

The Accu-cast B500 bridge has adjustable saddles for variable string spacing by +/- 1.5mm, answering every bassist’s specific string spacing needs.

Medium frets

Medium sized frets enhance the accuracy of your notes.

Ibanez Custom Electronics 3-band EQ w/EQ bypass switch & Mid frequency switch

Ibanez Custom Electronics 3-band EQ is our latest refinement on the concept of simple, straightforward, no-nonsense EQ control. The Boost and Cut for the Mid Control are each centered on slightly different frequencies, an innovation that delivers a preset range of the most favorable bass tones.
Precision tonal control, including a switch to choose passive-only operation. When the EQ bypass is activated, the treble tone control knob becomes the global tone control for the passive circuit.

item.title: Ovation Magnum II 1978 - 1980 - Red — $3,327.04
item.description:
$3,327.04 · Very Good · Ben Penna

For sale, this beautiful late 70s Ovation Magnum II, original Red finish.

In very good condition. Everything works perfectly, and sounds enormous. Has some dents on the body and back of the neck (see photos, it’s all here), but it’s more than 50-years old. And I just noticed after taking the pictures but the bit of pick guard on the upper right, hiding the batteries for the preamp, is just loose here, not broken. I couldn’t figure out how to close it properly with the peculiar big screw.


Very unique and bold design-wise/ sound-wise.
Still, pretty versatile as the two pickups are completely different entities on their own: the big neck PU being huge, boomy and bassy; the bridge PU quite trebly, spanky, precise. And of course both can be combined in the middle position for a large array of possibilities.
Plus the integrated active preamp works very well, and those sliders look so cool.

You want a unique, idiosyncratic, under the radar cult piece of bass?
The Magnum II is for you.

Comes with quality hard case, non-original.

Specs: (taken from the Owner's Manual)

  • OVATION MAGNUM II BASS 1978-80 w/OHSC
  • Body: Double-cutaway Honduras Mahogany in original "Red" finish
  • 1-piece mahogany neck (w/3 carbon graphite reinforcement bars)
  • Original Ovation machine heads
  • Ebony fingerboard w/20 frets & dot position markers
  • Nut: 41 mm
  • Built-in preamp powered by two 9V batteries (Supplied!)
  • 3-band graphic equalizer ±12 dB with individual controls for bass, midrange (400 Hz), and treble,
  • Pickups:
    - Neck: 4-coil humbucker w/individual volume controls for each string
    - Bridge: 2-coil w/large U-shaped pole pieces
  • Master volume control
  • Original bridge w/micro-adjustable brass saddles
  • Built-in foam mute (activated by slide bar) for upright bass-like tone (New foam)
  • Weight: 4.9kg / 10.8 lbs

Sell Your Gear

All our 2nd hand items are fully checked and setup in advance of shipping. We package very well and usually ship next working day unless the item is marked Collection Only. If the item is marked as Collection Only you can still reserve with a deposit using our reserve and collect facility at checkout or by contacting us.

item.title: Fender Jazz Bass 1964 - Sunburst — $17,768.05
item.description:
$17,768.05 · Good · Peters

1964 Fender Jazz Bass with original sunburst finish. Rosewood fretboard with clay dot inlays and tortoiseshell pickguard (the pickguard tips are missing). Except for three of the four screws holding the metal pickup and bridge covers, everything is original. Original hardshell case included.

The finish shows signs of wear, mainly on the body edges and with some light belt marks on the back.

The neck is in fantastic condition with only minor playing marks. The headstock is also clean.

This bass is a joy to play and offers a superb, consistent tone across the entire fretboard.

The neck is slim at the nut, and the truss rod has plenty of play.

Weight: 4 kg. Serial number: L321xx.

The neck dates from May 1964 (May 64A), the potentiometers from the 26th week of 1964.

Nut width: 37.75 mm. Body thickness at the 1st fret: 20.4 mm.

German CITES certificate is available.

item.title: Peavey T-40 with Maple Fretboard 1978 - 1988 - Natural inkl. Original Hardcase — $1,860.28
item.description:
$1,860.28 · Good · Boutique

Zum Verkauf steht ein Peavey T-40 aus vermutlich ca. 1980.

Der Bass befindet sich technisch in einem einwandfreien Zustand und lässt sich hervorragend spielen. Der Hals ist gerade, der Trussrod funktioniert einwandfrei und das Instrument ist sauber eingestellt. Elektronik und Hardware arbeiten zuverlässig – insgesamt ein voll funktionsfähiger T-40, der sofort einsatzbereit ist.

Optisch weist der Bass seinem Alter entsprechend Gebrauchsspuren auf. Es gibt verschiedene Kratzer, Schrammen und kleinere Macken, die den Vintage-Charakter unterstreichen, die Funktion oder Bespielbarkeit jedoch in keiner Weise beeinträchtigen. Bitte die Fotos zur Beurteilung des optischen Zustands beachten.

Wichtiger Hinweis: Ein Vorbesitzer hatte den Bass auf eine aktive Elektronik umgebaut. Daher befindet sich auf der Rückseite noch ein Batteriefach. Dieser Umbau wurde jedoch vollständig rückgängig gemacht, sodass der Bass heute wieder mit der originalen passiven Elektronik betrieben wird. Das Batteriefach ist lediglich ein optisches Relikt des früheren Umbaus.

Das original Peavey Hardcase ist inklusive. Allerdings hat dieses über die Jahre sein Innenfutter verloren.

item.title: 【used】Freedom Custom Guitar Research / C.S. Anthra 5st 4.22kg #23046045【GIB Hyogo】【Plek-adjusted】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $4,086
item.description:
$4,086 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

This product has been leveled using PLEK.

The string height at the 12th fret will be 1.5mm for the G string and 2.0mm for the E string (2.3mm for the Low-B string) upon shipment.

Freedom is a Tokyo-based guitar workshop that has gained overwhelming support from professional musicians in Japan, and in recent years, its name has begun to resonate overseas.

Originally highly regarded for its repair work, they now focus on manufacturing, and their creations are loved by many musicians for their high quality and rational design sense.

All basses from this brand are manufactured in Tokyo.

Through strict quality control, the wood used is carefully selected, and any wood that does not meet the standards is not used in the products.

Their commitment to a "100-year warranty" demonstrates their overwhelming confidence in quality and durability.

We believe that this level of quality control is possible precisely because it is a small workshop.

In that sense, the quality is fundamentally different from that of products made in large factories.

This model, the Anthra, is based on the traditional JB style, but with a unique interpretation that updates the body balance and playability for modern times.

It features classic specifications: an ash body and a maple fingerboard.

An ultra-thin finish maximizes the natural resonance of the wood, directly contributing to the rich vibration quality.

Furthermore, high-precision fretwork and meticulous construction result in a smooth playing feel that minimizes player stress.

It is equipped with an original design pickup.

It achieves a balance between a clear, crisp attack and a powerful, full-bodied nuance.

Overall, it boasts a wide range and extremely fast response, yet possesses a tight vibration that supports the foundation of the ensemble—a truly distinctive feature.

From the crisp, cutting highs when slapping to the thick bottom end when fingerpicking, it can be brought out exactly as the player desires, depending on their touch.

This instrument is of exceptionally high quality, a masterpiece that is JB-like while meeting more modern demands.

Fret wear is approximately 80%.
The truss rod is in good condition.
The neck is in good condition.
There is some paint peeling near the 4th string nut on the headstock.
The electronics are in good condition.
No obvious modifications were observed.
Controls: Neck pickup volume, bridge pickup volume, master tone
Nut width: 46.3mm
Bridge string spacing: 19mm
Scale length: 34 inches
Year of manufacture: Unknown
Country of manufacture: JAPAN

This guitar will be delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by a GIB craftsman.

item.title: Fender Musicmaster Bass 1978 — USA Vintage — $1,848.35
item.description:
$1,848.35 · Excellent · Joan's Gear Locker

Rare 1978 Fender Musicmaster Bass made in Fullerton, California. One of the last USA-made Musicmaster basses before Fender changed production. All original — original pickup, tuners, bridge, and electronics. Nothing has been modified or replaced.


Condition is excellent for its age. Shows minor cosmetic wear consistent with a 45-year-old vintage instrument — small marks and light playing wear — but no cracks, repairs, or structural issues. Neck is straight and plays well. Electronics work perfectly with no issues.


A true collector’s piece and a unique short-scale bass with that unmistakable vintage USA Fender character. Increasingly hard to find in this condition and originality.


Ships safely packed from Spain.

item.title: 【used】ZON / SONUS 4/2 3.834kg #120604【GIB Hyogo】【Plek-adjusted】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $5,964
item.description:
$5,964 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

This item has been leveled using PLEK.

The string height at the 12th fret is 1.5mm for the G string and 2.0mm for the E string (2.3mm for the Low-B string).

ZON is a bass brand established in 1981.

Its designs, which actively incorporate new materials such as graphite and phenowood, are highly regarded and are favored by musicians such as tetsuya of L'Arc-en-Ciel and Michael Manring. (Titles omitted)

ZON has several models, but this is the SONUS, which incorporates a classic style.

Unlike the LEGACY, which has a set-neck construction, the SONUS has a bolt-on neck joint.

It has a quick response and attack, and can be said to be a model that consciously aims for a somewhat Fender-like sound.

The body top uses beautiful figured maple, and the body back uses 2-piece alder.

The neck is made of carbon fiber, and the fingerboard is phenowood, a combination typical of the brand.

Phenowood is a wood laminate made by impregnating multiple layers of wood with black dye and a hardening resin, then compressing it at high heat.

The wood's expansion and contraction rate is extremely small, resulting in stability far superior to that of ordinary wood.

The pickups are Bartolini 72MV5K, and the preamp is a Bartolini NTMB.

While producing a clear and transparent sound, it never sounds inorganic; the combination with high-quality wood materials results in a sound rich in harmonics.

The quick and crisp response produced by the carbon neck and phenowood fingerboard is another major attraction.

This guitar possesses a truly refined balance while simultaneously exuding a noble impression. Why not consider it this time?

The frets have approximately 90% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck shows some upward bowing, but with the string height adjusted to 2.5mm for the G string and 3.0mm for the E string at the 12th fret, it is playable without any problems.

The body and paintwork have scattered dents and buckle scratches.

The electronics are in good condition.

The only clear modifications are the trimmer and the middle frequency switch, which have been replaced by GIB.

Controls: Master Volume, Balancer, Bass, Treble, Middle Frequency Switch, Middle
Nut Width: 40.4mm
Bridge String Spacing: 19.5mm
Scale Length: 34 Inch
Year of Manufacture: Unknown
Country of Manufacture: US

item.title: 【new】Sire / F10 Dx 5 HL NT 4.28kg #2N26H90034【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $3,099
item.description:
$3,099 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Sire became famous for releasing basses supervised by Marcus Miller. (Titles omitted)

The quality of the guitars and basses manufactured by the company is extremely high, and they are widely supported not only by Miller's fans but also by many others.

This is the F10dx headless 5-string model, which pursues the highest level of modern style within the brand, which is strongly associated with the JB style.

The body top is made of Spalted Maple with a beautiful grain, and the back is made of lightweight Swamp Ash with a wide tonal range.

The single-cut body shape not only has an elegant look, but also creates rich sustain by increasing the contact area between the neck and body.

The neck is a 5-piece through-neck construction combining Hard Maple and Mahogany.

This method of laminating multiple pieces of wood to create the neck reduces left-right differences and individual differences, resulting in higher rigidity and stability.

It uses a 35-inch scale, which enhances the tension in the low-end and improves pitch clarity.

Furthermore, the headless design is appealing because it improves upon the head dive often seen in multi-string basses and those with longer scales.

It boasts an exceptionally well-balanced body and excellent handling, ensuring stress-free playing even during extended sessions.

The fretboard is made of ebony.

The relatively flat 12-inch radius fretboard and fast-responding stainless steel frets maintain a consistent playing feel even in the higher positions.

It consistently delivers clear articulation even in modern playing techniques such as slap bass and fast phrases.

The fretboard features an edgeless design with rounded edges, fitting the playing style of players who prioritize smooth fingering.

Most players will likely find this feature appealing.

The hardware includes an independent "Sire Headless Individual Bass Bridge."

This directly transmits the vibrations of each string to the body, resulting in excellent sustain, clarity, and stable tuning.

The guitar features original humbucking pickups with wooden covers, the Marcus Custom-H Revolution Set.

A variation switch allows you to switch the pickup wiring, enabling you to bring out a wide range of characteristics, from thick, powerful humbucker tones to clear, articulate single-coil-like sounds.

The preamp is the Marcus Heritage-3 HD, boasting a wide range and clear output.

The combination of a 3-band EQ with a middle frequency and the pickup variation switch allows for a very wide range of sound shaping.

The sound is characterized by a deep, tight low end and rich sustain.

The response is quick, and the tone is remarkably clear and high-resolution.

While possessing the powerful push characteristic of humbuckers, the separation of each frequency band is excellent, and the sound remains clear even down to the Low-B.

The rich harmonics and stable pitch make it highly suitable for technical soloing and modern ensembles where clarity is paramount.

This is an excellent instrument that perfectly matches styles that want to fully utilize the rich, deep sound characteristic of high-end basses, the comfortable playability of a headless design, and the wide tonal range of a multi-string bass.

SPECIFICATION

-BODY-
Body Material: Swamp Ash with Spalted Maple Top
Body Shape: Deluxe Modern Single Cut Bass Shape
Body Color: NT (Natural)

-NECK-
Neck Material: Hard Maple / Mahogany 5-pc
Neck Shape: C-Shape
Scale: 35" (5-String)
Fingerboard Material: Ebony with Edgeless™ (Rolled Fretboard Edges)
Fingerboard Radius: 12"
Frets: 2.4mm Medium, 24 Stainless Frets
String Nut: Graphtech Black Tusq Nut, 45mm (5-String) Width
Inlay: WH Pearloid / Abalone Rounded-rectangular Block
Neck Joint: Neck Thru

-ELECTRONICS-
Pickup: Marcus Custom-H Revolution Set (Wooden Cover)
Preamp: Marcus Heritage-3 HD
Controls: Volume / Tone, Blender, Treble, Middle/Freq, Bass, Pickup Variation SW
Knobs: Aluminum Knob Black
-HARDWARE-
Bridge: Sire Headless Individual Bass Bridge
Tuning Gear: None
Hardware Finish: Black Satin
Pickguard: None
-CASE-
HARDFORM CASE

Delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.

Accessories: Gig bag, warranty card, wrench, finger ramp

item.title: 【new】Stem / Classic Style J5 Metallic purple Aged 4.23kg #230【GIB Hyogo】【Plek-adjusted】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $3,168
item.description:
$3,168 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

This guitar has been leveled using PLEK, and will be shipped with a string height of 1.5mm to 2.0mm.

Stem is a brand produced by Sago New Material Guitars, a handmade brand from the Kansai region of Japan.

Stem is positioned between Sago, which specializes in custom-order production, and Seed, which achieves high quality through excellent quality control while keeping costs down by manufacturing overseas.

Stem is known for producing models that retain Sago's high quality while keeping costs down by fixing specifications and shapes.

This model is the Stem version of Sago's flagship model, the Classic Style J5.

The body is made of alder, the fingerboard is rosewood, and the neck is hard maple, a classic wood combination.

The finish is Sago's signature thin lacquer.

Furthermore, it has been given a realistic aged finish, giving it an outstanding look.

The staff who performed this aging process were formerly Fender craftsmen and were part of the team of the highly acclaimed master builder, Carlos Lopez.

Carlos's team primarily focused on relicing work, and their work was highly regarded both inside and outside the company.

The pickups are original L(x) Lite BJ5s.

These pickups are known for their thick, full-bodied, rich midrange.

The tuning keys are Gotoh GB528s.

The hardware has also been aged, and combined with the body's "Aged" finish, it exudes the look and feel of a vintage guitar that has been played for many years.

The sound has the open nuance characteristic of Stem guitars.

While featuring a strong, punchy low-mid range, it boasts a very well-balanced output from low to high frequencies, resulting in a straightforward and easy-to-use sound.

Stem is not simply a cheaper version of Sago.

With its outstanding paint quality, honed through training at Fender Custom Shop, and flexibility focused on high-demand orders, it holds its own against numerous high-end models.

It's a serious brand packed with value far exceeding its price.

Please give it a try.

SPECS

Body: Alder 2P
Neck: Hard Maple (Bolt-on)
Fingerboard: Rosewood
Neck Pickup: L(x) Lite BJ5
Bridge Pickup: L(x) Lite BJ5
Pickguard: Brown Tortoise 4P
Control: 2 Volume + 1 Tone
Scale: 864mm (21 Frets)
Tuner: Gotoh GB528
Bridge: JB Bridge
Hardware Color: Chrome
Color: Metallic Purple
Finish: Thin Lacquer Heavy Aged
Strings: SIT 045-125
Accessory: Gig Bag, Tool Set

Text: Kaigo Kawami

Product Photos: Muragishi

item.description:
$2,000 · Excellent · The Guitar Witch

Here is a lovely and quite rare bass. Most vintage Maton instruments don't seem to leave Australia very often. The Fyrbyrd guitar is the most well known of the vintage Maton instruments, but everything they made was unique and very functional.

This bass plays wonderfully and has a broad range of tones with the two pickups and 3 way selector. They are also quite well placed and it's a very useful instrument for a wide range of styles.

This bass was also formerly owned and used by legendary musician Jeff Tweedy (of Wilco).

Please get in touch with any questions! Truss rod works, frets are good, neck is straight, no issues. Comes with the original green hardshell case.

item.title: FENDER USA American Vintage '62 Jazz Bass, 2-Knob, 3-Color Sunburst [V092832] (07/07) — $1,996.48
item.description:
$1,996.48 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

The classic American vintage '62 Jazz Bass!
This iconic Fender electric bass features the classic '62-style stacked knobs. It boasts an alder body, a maple neck, a slab-jointed rosewood fingerboard, and dot position markers.
While retaining vintage-style elements such as reverse-winding tuners and a lacquer finish, this instrument is characterized by its familiar single-coil pickups and the classic alder body/rosewood fingerboard combination?a timeless tone that still resonates today.
There are minor dents and playwear throughout the instrument, with wear on the headstock logo and numerous dents on the top of the headstock and the back of the neck.
The entire guitar has been relacquered; the neck shows slight bowing, the truss rod has little travel remaining, and the frets are low, but there are currently no playing issues such as buzzing.
Additionally, there is some tarnish and plating wear on the metal parts, as well as rust on the bridge saddles.
The rear tone capacitor has been replaced.

item.title: FENDER 1972 Jazz Bass 3-Color Sunburst [373429] (07/07) — $4,587.39
item.description:
$4,587.39 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

A 1972 Jazz Bass that combines a refined sound with a rich, vintage look has just arrived.

This instrument features striking aging that is both pronounced yet natural. The neck date is faded and unreadable, so we determined it was made in 1972 based on the serial number.
The pickups have been replaced with cross-wound black-bobbin models?likely made by Seymour Duncan?while the pots were replaced with 1978 models, the jack with a Switchcraft unit, and the capacitor with a Cornell Dubilier unit. Although most of the electronics have been replaced, the result is a warm, well-balanced, and clear tone that unmistakably retains the sound of the early '70s. Judging by the sound alone, it's on par with fully original instruments from the same era.
We refretted the guitar and replaced the nut upon its arrival at our shop, making it incredibly comfortable to play. If you're looking to enjoy playing this guitar for many years to come, you won't find a better example in better condition.

We highly recommend that players looking for a guitar from this era give this one a try.

Weight: 4.14 kg
Comes with original hard case

Condition Grade: B+: Some minor scratches, but no issues for normal use

A used 2025 American Vintage II PB has arrived!!

Introducing the American Vintage II series?an extremely accurate recreation of iconic, innovative instruments!

The American Vintage II series offers extremely accurate reproductions of the innovative electric guitars and basses that changed the history of music.

Featuring body shapes, necks, and hardware configurations that faithfully reflect the original specifications, along with premium finishes and pickups voiced for each specific era with meticulous attention to detail, each instrument encapsulates the essence of authentic Fender craftsmanship and tone.

item.title: Riverhead RMC-700 WHITE [002558] (07/07) — $439.36
item.description:
$439.36 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

A Riverhead Warlock bass-type model manufactured at the Headway factory from the mid-1980s onward.
Body: Alder Neck: Maple Fingerboard: Rosewood Machine Heads: GOTOH Equipped with two PB-style pickups. Flipping the mini switch activates only the lower section of the front pickup and the upper section of the rear pickup.

The truss rod is at its limit, showing neck twist and forward bow. The frets are generally low and flattened. At present, this has minimal impact on playability. The instrument will be delivered as-is.
The mini switch has been replaced, and the frets have been leveled.
The 1st string action adjustment screw has been replaced with a non-original part.
Cracking in the finish is present at the neck joint. There are scratches and dents on the neck back accompanied by indentations, creating a noticeable bumpy feel.
Overall paint discoloration is visible. The headstock and body show numerous scratches, dents, and paint chips. Metal parts exhibit noticeable rust, tarnish, and pitting.
Neck Condition: Twist and forward bow
Truss rod: Limited travel in both tightening and loosening directions
Fret wear: 50-60% remaining
Case: Soft case / Genuine
Accessories: Locking pins (attached to body)
Condition Grade: B: Significant scratches & signs of use, but no issues for normal playing

item.title: Fender Japan JB62M Black [MIJ T077362] (07/07) — $1,014.39
item.description:
$1,014.39 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

Based on the classic JB62 style, the JB62M has been redesigned with a 32-inch medium scale.
This practical model from Fender Japan's later production period combines excellent playability with a traditional Jazz Bass sound.

This particular instrument, bearing the MIJ serial number T077362, is believed to have been manufactured around 2007?2010. It is a medium-scale model, as confirmed by the JB62-MID stamp on the neck joint. The neck pocket retains the original JB62 VWH engraving, along with traces of a correction to BLK.

Featuring the classic configuration of an alder body (or similar), a maple neck, and a rosewood fingerboard, it is equipped with two Fender Japan-made JB single-coil pickups.Despite its simple 2-volume/1-tone layout, it delivers a well-defined attack and the characteristic range of a jazz bass. The 32-inch scale?shorter than the standard 34 inches?results in slightly lower string tension and a more compact fingerboard range. It offers a natural feel that's well-suited for extended playing sessions or for bassists switching from a guitar.
There are some minor scratches and dents, but no particularly noticeable damage.
The neck is reverse-bowed on the G-string side and slightly twisted. The frets have been leveled, and there are currently no issues with playability.
Condition Grade: B+: Some minor scratches, but no issues for normal use

Serial Number: MIJ T077362
Year of Manufacture: 2007?2010
Country of Manufacture: Japan
Case: Soft case
Weight: 3.76 kg

Fret Wear: Within normal range; fret height is sufficient for playing

String action (treble side): 2.0 mm at the 12th fret
Nut width: 38.3 mm

Strings at Setup: D'Addario EXL170M / .045-.100 set

item.title: CoolZ ZJB-10R Vintage White [L170716] (07/07) — $503.97
item.description:
$503.97 · Very Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

This instrument retains the classic contours of a jazz bass while incorporating the meticulous craftsmanship unique to Japanese-made instruments.

Launched as a music store's original brand, this model is a solidly crafted, orthodox JB-style instrument backed by Fujigen's high precision manufacturing. It features a classic alder body with a maple neck and rosewood fingerboard, which, combined with simple 2-volume/1-tone controls, delivers an easy-to-play, natural tone balance.

Intonation stability provided by the Circle Fretting System (C.F.S.) and clear attack are hallmarks of this series. Despite being in the low-to-mid price range, it offers a high level of playability and is built to withstand the rigors of live performance.
While there are a few minor scratches, the guitar is in good condition overall with minimal signs of use.

item.title: Fender American Original '70s Jazz Bass with Maple Fretboard 2018 - 2022 - 3-Color Sunburst — $2,199
item.description:
$2,199 · Excellent · JJ’s Bassment

2018 Fender American Original 70s Jazz Bass in excellent condition. Hang tag says 75, which makes sense with the 4 bolt neck plate. Beautiful three tone sunburst that plays and sounds amazing. A Fender Hi Mass bridge has been added with zero modification holes. The original bridge will come with the bass. The neck has a binded pearloid block maple fretboard, with the classic U shape. The original knobs and strap horns (pictured) will be shipped on the bass. The chrome barrel knobs and strap lock horns are not included. Comes with the chrome bridge and neck pu covers. COA and the rest of the case candy pictured is included. Ships in its original case. This is without a doubt the best Jazz bass I’ve owned, but I have too many and she needs to be played regularly. All questions are welcomed, and I only ship within the US. Thanks for looking!
